item.title: Stalked woman shot at 14 times by ex-boyfriend during horrific 911 call
item.description: Gloria Choi and her friends called Lakewood, Washington, 911 four times in 48 hours to report her being stalked by an ex-boyfriend. Two days later, he ran her off the road and riddled her truck with bullets as she was on the line with a 911 dispatcher.

item.title: The Love Bombing of Gloria Choi
item.description: A dream romance ends in murder after a woman falls for a stranger. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports.

item.title: NFL whiff leads to lifesaving diagnosis for sales manager at Kentucky Derby
item.description: Attending this year's Kentucky Derby meant more for thoroughbred expert Mark Toothaker, who suffered a seizure from laughing at a whiffed NFL field goal attempt that led to a lifesaving diagnosis.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after failed effort at government rescue deal
item.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

item.title: Vehicle carrying explosives crashes into Portland athletic club, driver dead
item.description: A vehicle carrying explosives crashed through the front entrance of an athletic club in downtown Portland, Oregon. The driver was killed.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Trump says he is reviewing new Iran peace proposal
item.description: President Trump told reporters Saturday he is reviewing a new 14-point peace proposal that was submitted by Iran.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 3)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Golden Tempo wins 152nd Kentucky Derby, making history for its trainer
item.description: Cherie DeVaux became the first woman to train the winner of the opening leg of the Triple Crown.

item.title: What will a Spirit Airlines shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights as the budget airline ceases operations.

item.title: Drugmaker asks Supreme Court to halt ruling blocking online access to mifepristone
item.description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone​ asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier.

item.title: Meet the Kentucky Derby horses who ran in the 2026 race
item.description: The Kentucky Derby saw a field of 18 horses Saturday in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: Mountain lion cub with missing toes rescued after being discovered alone in California
item.description: A mountain lion cub missing some toes was found alone in the Santa Monica Mountains at just three weeks old. Crimson is now being cared for at the Oakland Zoo, and has clawed his way into people's hearts. Itay Hod has the story.

item.title: Marine heatwave off California threatens extreme weather events
item.description: The ocean off California keeps breaking heat records. Experts say it could produce a stormy summer that may threaten lives. Max Darrow explains.

item.title: States scramble to redistrict after Supreme Court limits Voting Rights Act
item.description: Alabama and Tennessee are the latest states rushing to redraw congressional districts after a Supreme Court ruling that further weakens the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Abortion pill maker asks Supreme Court to restore access for those who receive it by mail
item.description: A drugmaker of mifepristone filed an emergency appeal to the Supreme Court to restore access for Americans who receive the abortion pill by mail.

item.title: Trump says U.S. Navy is "sort of like pirates" in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump compared the U.S. Navy to pirates, as it enforces the blockade of Iranian ports near the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. also warned shipping companies that they could face penalties for paying tolls to Iran to safely pass through the critical waterway. Ramy Inocencio has more.

item.title: Rising oil prices cause political headache for Trump administration
item.description: Saturday marked 61 days since the start of the war with Iran, and gas prices continue to soar. Olivia Gazis reports on how rising prices are causing new political issues for President Trump.

item.title: No more runway for Spirit Airlines as budget carrier shuts down
item.description: Discount carrier Spirit Airlines ceased operations on Saturday. It had been struggling with skyrocketing fuel costs and mountains of debt. A proposed government bailout plan unraveled, leaving thousands of passengers, employees and investors in limbo. Ali Bauman reports from Newark's Liberty International Airport.

item.title: 5/2: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Spirit Airlines collapses, stranding passengers; gas prices keep surging across the country.

item.title: Kentucky Derby brings eye-catching looks, from statement hats to standout suits
item.description: Held every spring in Louisville, Kentucky, the event is also known for its over-the-top hats and vibrant suits and dresses.

item.title: Ford recalls more than 179,000 vehicles over front seat defect
item.description: Ford Motor Company is recalling over 179,000 vehicles due to a front seat issue that can increase the risk of injury in a crash, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said.

item.title: Jewish community in U.K. on high alert after Golders Green knife attack
item.description: Jewish community members in the U.K. are on high alert after two men were wounded in a stabbing attack in London's Golders Green neighborhood in what officials called an antisemitic terrorist attack.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after failing to secure government bailout
item.description: Spirit Airlines announced early Saturday morning that it would immediately cease operations after failing to secure a last-minute government bailout.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's latest peace proposal as gas prices skyrocket
item.description: President Trump rejected Iran's latest proposal for a peace deal on Friday, saying he was "not satisfied." Meanwhile, gas prices have hit a four-year high, according to AAA.

item.title: After a fire torched their Nebraska ranch, anonymous donors came to their rescue
item.description: Mike and Kayla Wintz lost their entire 11,000-acre ranch to a wildfire in the span of about two hours. They have since been gifted about $80,000 worth of hay, mostly from anonymous donors.

item.title: ICE reports 18th detainee death in 2026, putting agency on track for new record
item.description: ICE reported the 18th death of an individual in its custody so far this year, putting the agency on track to record a new all-time high in detainee deaths.

item.title: Pickleball Inc. $225 million deal creates new home for sport
item.description: The deal merged Major League Pickleball and the Carvana PPA Tour, two of the nascent sport's most active entities, under one company, Pickleball Inc.

item.title: Trump administration to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said earlier this week he was reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, which hosts more than 35,000 U.S. service members.

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.
